objective: Fix remaining RunPod serverless startup failures by requiring a guarded CUDA usability probe before treating CUDA as available.
scope: Apply a minimal follow-up runtime fix in src/engine_args.py: keep existing fast CUDA checks, add guarded CUDA init probe (current_device/get_device_properties) with RuntimeError/Exception handling, preserve explicit DEVICE override behavior, and keep multi-GPU auto behavior only for confirmed usable CUDA.
acceptance_criteria: When DEVICE is explicitly set, engine args keep that value with no automatic override.
When DEVICE is not explicitly set and CUDA runtime is unavailable (is_available false, device_count 0, or guarded CUDA usability probe raises), engine args set device=cpu and log why.
When CUDA runtime is available, device auto behavior remains unchanged and no CPU forcing occurs.
Automatic multi-GPU tensor_parallel_size adjustment happens only when CUDA runtime is available and GPU count > 1.
rollback_plan: Fork-local rollback is a single commit revert of src/engine_args.py and this request file, restoring previous CUDA detection behavior if probe-related false negatives or startup regressions are observed.
deploy_plan: Deploy with conservative rollout 1->2->4; verify logs show probe-based CPU fallback in serverless checks with broken CUDA init and unchanged GPU path when CUDA probe succeeds.
threat_model: Primary risk is false-negative CUDA detection causing lower performance from CPU fallback; mitigated by keeping fast checks unchanged, only adding guarded init verification, preserving explicit DEVICE overrides, and logging precise probe failure reasons.
